perturbation.original_text: Network Management Outsourcing Agreement Datec Contact Details [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****]
perturbation.changed_text: Network Management Outsourcing Agreement Vendor Contact Details [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****] [*****]
perturbation.explanation: Changed 'Datec' to 'Vendor' under the assumption that Datec is the vendor in this contract. This introduces ambiguity as 'Datec' is used throughout the document, and this change implies 'Vendor' and 'Datec' may refer to different entities. This specifically creates confusion in later sections that specify Datec's responsibilities, such as section 2.1 where Datec's Deliverables are explained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Datec will: (a) provide operational support services for designated Equipment and Networks; (b) perform moves, adds and changes (MACs) for data services; (c) provide pricing and perform MACs for data services; (d) take over where agreed and/or manage existing third party contracts (MTP'S) as agreed with the BSP; (e) act as the local BSP representative in matters directly related to the BSP, in a cooperative manner, with other Managed Third Parties ("MTP") and take responsibility for the resolution of WAN related activity by acting as the vendor interface. (f) provide hardware maintenance support services for designated Equipment (break Fix) as defined in Schedule A, Datec will deliver the following project related Services: (a) perform Site Surveys, Stage and configure new Equipment, deliver the Equipment, install the Equipment, manage the installation of the Network Services, test, and accept the Network as required.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Datec' to 'Vendor' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text specifies 'Datec' as the entity responsible for certain obligations, as seen in the 'Contradicted Text' where 'Datec will: (a) provide operational support services...'. By changing 'Datec' to 'Vendor' in the 'Changed Text', the contract creates ambiguity about who is responsible for these obligations. The contract now uses two terms, 'Datec' and 'Vendor', possibly interchangeably, without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations as defined in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. This directly contradicts the sections specifying Datec's responsibilities, creating uncertainty and potentially unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Datec (PNG) LTD Deliverables 2.1 General Statements Datec will: (a) provide operational support services for designated Equipment and Networks; (b) perform moves, adds and changes (MACs) for data services; (c) provide pricing and perform MACs for data services; (d) take over where agreed and/or manage existing third party contracts (MTP'S) as agreed with the BSP; (e) act as the local BSP representative in matters directly related to the BSP, in a cooperative manner, with other Managed Third Parties ("MTP") and take responsibility for the resolution of WAN related activity by acting as the vendor interface. (f) provide hardware maintenance support services for designated Equipment (break Fix) as defined in Schedule A, Datec will deliver the following project related Services: (a) perform Site Surveys, Stage and configure new Equipment, deliver the Equipment, install the Equipment, manage the installation of the Network Services, test, and accept the Network as required.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor (PNG) LTD Deliverables 2.1 General Statements Contractor will: (a) provide operational support services for designated Equipment and Networks; (b) perform moves, adds and changes (MACs) for data services; (c) provide pricing and perform MACs for data services; (d) take over where agreed and/or manage existing third party contracts (MTP'S) as agreed with the BSP; (e) act as the local BSP representative in matters directly related to the BSP, in a cooperative manner, with other Managed Third Parties ("MTP") and take responsibility for the resolution of WAN related activity by acting as the vendor interface. (f) provide hardware maintenance support services for designated Equipment (break Fix) as defined in Schedule A, Contractor will deliver the following project related Services: (a) perform Site Surveys, Stage and configure new Equipment, deliver the Equipment, install the Equipment, manage the installation of the Network Services, test, and accept the Network as required.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Datec' with 'Contractor' causes ambiguity because 'Datec' is still used throughout the rest of the agreement. This now raises questions about which entity, Datec or the Contractor, is responsible for the deliverables outlined in section 2.1, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This creates a contradiction in the entire file, where Datec and Contractor are used interchangeably without defining them as the same entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Datec will: 2.3.1 Maintain designated Equipment as listed in Schedule A, and any other equipment that may added from time to time within Papua New Guinea. (i) BSP will provide Datec with tine required authority to acquire spares parts in order to complete any repairs /replacement that may be required (ii) Datec will be responsible for the provision of any spare parts required to complete any repairs to the managed Network equipment. 2.3.2 Maintain a Single Point of Contact to interface with and coordinate problem determination and resolution with BSP's appropriate support personnel and third party service providers; 2.3.3 Perform Incident Management to closure. Datec will provide Level 1 support by: (a) entering problem information into a problem record; (b) performing incident source identification and severity impact level; (c) providing feedback to users; (d) invoking proper incident resolution resources; (e) dispatching on site service resources, where applicable; (f) monitoring resolution status and document actions taken; (g) manage escalation or critical situation procedures; (h) compiling and maintain the incident resolution system; (i) confirming incident resolution with the end user prior to closure; (j) maintaining a second and third-level "contacts list" system including site access requirements for business and after hours site access;
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Datec' with 'Contractor' in section 2.1, while the contradicted text refers exclusively to 'Datec' for specific responsibilities such as equipment maintenance, single point of contact, and incident management. This creates a direct conflict because it is unclear whether Datec or the Contractor is now responsible for these duties. The original and contradicted texts use 'Datec' to define the party responsible for these duties, while the changed text introduces 'Contractor'. This contradicts the continued use of 'Datec' in other sections of the document, leading to ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.15.1 provide a single-point-of-contact (SPOC) Help Desk. The Help Desk will support and have access to, and maintain, sufficient information that will facilitate knowledge of the BSP's business and technology environment; 2.15.2 provide one primary Help Desk contact phone number and enable contact fax, e-mail and browser for all technology requests. This includes, but is not limited to, communication faults, Hardware and Software failures, and general enquiries by the BSP.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.15.1 provide a single-point-of-contact (SPOC) Support Center. The Support Center will support and have access to, and maintain, sufficient information that will facilitate knowledge of the BSP's business and technology environment; 2.15.2 provide one primary Support Center contact phone number and enable contact fax, e-mail and browser for all technology requests. This includes, but is not limited to, communication faults, Hardware and Software failures, and general enquiries by the BSP.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Help Desk' to 'Support Center' without defining them as the same introduces a contradiction. Later references to 'Help Desk' in the document, such as in section 2.14, create confusion about which entity is responsible for those specific duties and tasks, Help desk or the Support Center.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a component of the base fee Datec will perform any required MACs that fall within the scope of work contained in this agreement. Datec will also be required to provide MACs to the BSP with pricing and costs for tasks that fall outside the agreement such as project management or the acquisition of new equipment or upgrades but not just restricted to these items. On approval of any MAC Datec will be required to assist in the supervision, management and implementation of changes related to the Network and the Services as necessary to satisfy BSP's business and operational requirements. Datec will: (a) receive, log and track the completion of service requests and provide related information to the IBM Help Desk and billing system; (b) schedule the execution of the MAC; (c) for MACs, dispatch and manage the performance of appropriate technicians; (d) coordinate and communicate with designated personnel concerning scheduling and requirements, so as to eliminate the business impact on end users; (e) provide the necessary technical support to complete the MAC; (f) physically move Equipment, as required, and install any necessary in-scope cabling where appropriate; (g) notify the BSP contact person of completion of the MAC; (h) confirm correct implementation of the MAC with the designated personnel and the BSP Help Desk, as appropriate; (i) track the completion of the MAC in a management system by updating relevant inventory and configuration information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Help Desk' to 'Support Center' without defining them as equivalent introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text explicitly references 'IBM Help Desk' for specific tasks (receiving, logging, tracking service requests, and confirming MAC implementations). Since the 'Help Desk' and 'Support Center' are now distinct entities due to the change in terminology, it's unclear which entity performs these specific duties, creating a direct contradiction and potentially impacting service delivery and accountability. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
